armed-conflicts.headline: Global conflicts intensify with strikes in Nigeria and high casualties in Ukraine.
armed-conflicts.summary: On December 25, 2025, the United States, in coordination with the Nigerian government, conducted strikes against Islamic State militants in northwest Nigeria, and deadly clashes were reported on the Tajik-Afghan border. The year 2025 saw heightened conflicts globally, with Ukraine experiencing a 66% increase in civilian casualties in December compared to the previous year, and ongoing high levels of violence in Gaza and Sudan. Multiple regions faced intensified fighting and increased civilian deaths throughout the year.

regions.name: Sokoto State, Nigeria
regions.detail: US and Nigerian forces conducted strikes against Islamic State militants in the region on December 25.

regions.name: Khorugh, Tajikistan (near Tajik-Afghan Border)
regions.detail: Deadly clashes were reported near the Tajik-Afghan border on December 25.

regions.name: Kyiv, Ukraine
regions.detail: Ukraine, including Kyiv, faced significantly increased civilian casualties throughout December.

regions.name: Gaza City, Palestine
regions.detail: Gaza City continued to experience ongoing high levels of violence on December 25.

regions.name: Khartoum, Sudan
regions.detail: Khartoum, Sudan, experienced ongoing high levels of violence on December 25.

human-rights.headline: Human rights abuses widespread, particularly against opposition MPs.
human-rights.summary: Throughout 2025, over 1,000 parliamentarians across 58 countries faced human rights abuses, with 83% involving opposition MPs, indicating a concerning trend of democratic backsliding. Afghanistan's crisis deepened with an accelerating erosion of fundamental rights, especially for women and girls. While there were positive developments like Saint Lucia decriminalizing same-sex conduct, repression of youth-led protests in Morocco and Nepal highlighted ongoing challenges to civic freedoms globally.

regions.name: Kabul, Afghanistan
regions.detail: Kabul, Afghanistan, faced an accelerating erosion of fundamental human rights, particularly for women and girls in 2025.

regions.name: Manila, Philippines
regions.detail: Manila represents a region where human rights abuses, especially against opposition MPs, were widespread in 2025.

regions.name: Rabat, Morocco
regions.detail: Rabat, Morocco, saw repression of youth-led protests, highlighting challenges to civic freedoms in 2025.

regions.name: Kathmandu, Nepal
regions.detail: Kathmandu, Nepal, faced repression of youth-led protests, highlighting challenges to civic freedoms in 2025.

regions.name: Gaza City, Palestine
regions.detail: Gaza City, Palestine, experienced widespread human rights abuses consistent with global trends in 2025.

climate-events.headline: 2025 among hottest years on record, marked by severe global climate events.
climate-events.summary: The year 2025 was on track to be the joint-second hottest year on record, with global average temperatures significantly above pre-industrial levels. Deadly "supercharged" storms in Southeast Asia, Hurricane Melissa in the Caribbean, and wildfires in Southern California underscored a year of extreme weather events. On December 25, 2025, a global weather hazards summary highlighted ongoing risks of flooding in Southern Africa and drought in Eastern Africa.

regions.name: Jakarta, Indonesia (representing Southeast Asia)
regions.detail: Jakarta, Indonesia, was affected by deadly "supercharged" storms throughout 2025, typical of Southeast Asia.

regions.name: Kingston, Jamaica
regions.detail: Kingston, Jamaica, experienced the impact of Hurricane Melissa, which affected the Caribbean in 2025.

regions.name: Addis Ababa, Ethiopia (representing Eastern Africa)
regions.detail: Addis Ababa, Ethiopia, faced ongoing risks of drought on December 25, representative of Eastern Africa.

regions.name: Los Angeles, USA
regions.detail: Los Angeles, USA, experienced wildfires, characteristic of Southern California's extreme weather in 2025.

regions.name: Delhi, India
regions.detail: Delhi, India, experienced a year among the hottest on record, marked by severe global climate events in 2025.

public-health.headline: Global health faces funding cuts and resurgent diseases despite some gains.
public-health.summary: Public health in 2025 was marked by a significant drop in global aid funding, disrupting health systems in vulnerable communities. The U.S. saw alarming resurgences of measles and whooping cough due to vaccine hesitancy, alongside concerns of a "quad-demic" of respiratory illnesses in some states. Conversely, there were notable victories, with Maldives achieving triple elimination of mother-to-child transmission of HIV, syphilis, and hepatitis B, and several African countries introducing malaria vaccines.

regions.name: Washington D.C., USA
regions.detail: Washington D.C. reflected the US trend of alarming resurgences of measles and whooping cough in 2025.

regions.name: Kabul, Afghanistan
regions.detail: Kabul's health systems were disrupted in 2025 due to a significant drop in global aid funding.

regions.name: Malé, Maldives
regions.detail: Malé, Maldives, celebrated the triple elimination of mother-to-child transmission of HIV, syphilis, and hepatitis B in 2025.

regions.name: Geneva, Switzerland (representing global health)
regions.detail: Geneva represented global health concerns of funding cuts and disrupted health systems in vulnerable communities in 2025.

economic-crises.headline: Global economy shows resilience amidst trade tensions and AI bubble fears.
economic-crises.summary: December 2025 saw global markets calm after a year of uncertainty and rising trade tensions, with central banks making decisive monetary policy shifts like the US Federal Reserve's third rate cut of the year. While the global economy showed resilience, underlying fragilities remained due to geopolitical uncertainty and concerns about an 'AI bubble'. China's Q3 GDP growth slipped due to weaknesses in the property sector and fading export momentum.

regions.name: New York City, USA
regions.detail: New York City's markets calmed in December after the US Federal Reserve's third rate cut of 2025.

regions.name: London, UK
regions.detail: London's global markets calmed in December 2025 after a year of uncertainty and trade tensions.

regions.detail: Frankfurt, representing the Eurozone, saw central banks make decisive monetary policy shifts in December 2025.

regions.name: San Francisco, USA (representing AI sector)
regions.detail: San Francisco, representing the AI sector, faced ongoing concerns about an 'AI bubble' in December 2025.

democratic-erosion.headline: Democratic backsliding and political abuses rise globally in 2025.
democratic-erosion.summary: Globally, 2025 witnessed significant democratic backsliding, with over 1,000 opposition parliamentarians in 58 countries experiencing human rights abuses. Risks of electoral violence and a hardline stance against dissent were noted in Uganda, while a coup attempt targeted President Patrice Talon in Benin. Myanmar continued to face acute insecurity under a military-imposed election, and protests in Iran were met with a high death toll.

regions.name: Geneva, Switzerland (representing IPU)
regions.detail: Geneva, representing the IPU, highlighted significant global democratic backsliding and abuses against MPs in 2025.

regions.name: Kampala, Uganda
regions.detail: Kampala, Uganda, noted risks of electoral violence and a hardline stance against dissent throughout 2025.

humanitarian.headline: Aid cuts deepen severe humanitarian crises in Sudan, Afghanistan, and Palestine.
humanitarian.summary: Global humanitarian crises deepened in 2025 due to record aid cuts, leaving millions in need. Sudan remained the worst humanitarian emergency, with 30 million requiring assistance amid conflict and aid blockades. Afghanistan faced a severe crisis with 22 million people needing help, 17.4 million food insecure, and widespread health facility closures due to massive funding cuts. The West Bank and Gaza continued to experience deepening humanitarian crises exacerbated by conflict, displacement, and harsh winter conditions.

regions.name: Khartoum, Sudan
regions.detail: Khartoum, Sudan, remained the worst humanitarian emergency with millions needing aid amid conflict and blockades.

regions.name: Kabul, Afghanistan
regions.detail: Kabul, Afghanistan, faced a severe humanitarian crisis with millions needing food and aid due to funding cuts.

regions.detail: Gaza City continued to experience a deepening humanitarian crisis exacerbated by conflict and harsh winter.
